1.1: The beginning of The Gospel of Yeshua The Messiah, The Son of God.
1.2: As it is written in Isaiah the Prophet, “Behold, I shall send my messenger before your presence to prepare your way.”
1.3: “A voice that cries in the wilderness, prepare the way of THE LORD JEHOVAH and make level his paths.”
1.4: Yohannan was in the wilderness, baptizing and preaching the baptism of repentance for the release of sins.
1.5: And all the country of Judea and all the people of Jerusalem were going out to him, and he baptized them in the Jordan when they confessed their sins.
1.6: But the same Yohannan was clothed with clothing of hair of the camel and he was bound with a belt of leather at his waist and his food was locusts and honey of the field.
1.7: And he was preaching and he said, “Behold, after me shall come he who is mightier than I, the strap of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop and to loose.
1.8: I have immersed you in water, but he will immerse you in The Spirit of Holiness.
1.9: And it was in those days; Yeshua came from Nazareth of Galilee and was immersed in the Jordan by Yohannan.
1.10: And at once, as he came up from the waters, he saw the heavens being ripped open and The Spirit who was descending upon him as a dove.
1.11: And there was a voice from the heavens: “You are my Son, The Beloved; with you I am delighted.”
1.12: And at once The Spirit drove him into the wilderness.
1.13: And he had been there in the wilderness forty days when he was tempted by Satan. And he was with the animals, and Angels were ministering to him.
1.14: After Yohannan was delivered up, Yeshua came to Galilee and was preaching The Gospel of the Kingdom of God.
1.15: And he said, “Time is coming to an end. The Kingdom of God has arrived. Repent and believe in The Good News.”
1.16: And as he walked around the Sea of Galilee, he saw Shimeon and his brother Andraeus who were casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.
1.17: And Yeshua said to them, “Come after me, and I will make you fishers of men.”
1.18: And at once they left their nets and went after him.
1.19: And when he passed by a little he saw Yaqob, son of Zebedee, and Yohannan his brother, and also those in the ship who were setting their nets in order.
1.20: And he called them and at once they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants and they went after him.
1.21: And when they entered Kapernahum, at once he taught in their synagogue on the Sabbath.
1.22: And they were dumbfounded at his teaching, for he was teaching them as one having authority and not like their Scribes.
1.23: And in their synagogue there was a man who had a vile spirit in him, and he cried out
1.24: And he said, “What business do we have with you, Yeshua the Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are, The Holy One of God.”
1.25: And Yeshua rebuked him and said, “Shut your mouth and come out of him.”
1.26: And the foul spirit threw him down and he cried out in a loud voice and came out of him.
1.27: And all of them marveled and they were inquiring with one another, saying, “What is this?”, and “What is this new teaching? For he commands even the foul spirits with authority and they obey him.”
1.28: And at once his fame went out in the whole region of Galilee.
1.29: And they went out from the synagogue and they came to the house of Shimeon and Andraeus with Yaqob and Yohannan.
1.30: And the Mother-in-law of Shimeon was lying ill with fever and they told him about her.
1.31: And he came near and took her by the hand and raised her up and at once her fever left her and she was waiting on them.
1.32: But in the evening at the going down of the sun they brought to him all of them who had been ill and demon possessed.
1.33: And the whole city was assembled at the door.
1.34: And he healed multitudes that had become ill with various diseases and he cast out many evil spirits. He did not allow them to speak because they did know him.
1.35: And in the very early morning he arose first and went on to a desolate place and there he prayed.
1.36: And Shimeon and his companions were seeking for him.
1.37: And when they found him they were all saying to him, “The people are all looking for you.”
1.38: He said to them, “Walk to the villages and the cities near to us, so that I also may preach there, for because of this I have come.”
1.39: And he was preaching in all their synagogues and in all Galilee and he cast out demons.
1.40: A leper came to him, fell at his feet, and begged him, saying, “If you are willing, you are able to make me clean.”
1.41: Yeshua was moved with compassion for him. reaching out his hand, he touched him and said, “I am willing; be cleansed.”
1.42: In that moment his leprosy went from him and he was cleansed.
1.43: Yeshua reproved him and sent him out.
1.44: He said to him, “See that you tell no man, but go and show yourself to the priest. Bring gifts for your cleansing, just as Moses commanded for their testimony.”
1.45: But when he went out he began preaching it much and he reported the event so that Yeshua was not able to openly enter the cities. He was in deserted places and they were coming to him from every place.

2.1: Yeshua again entered Kapernahum for some days. When they heard that he was in the house,
2.2: So many were assembled that it was not able to hold them before the door. He was speaking the word with them
2.3: When a paralytic was brought to him, being carried by four men.
2.4: Because they were not able to be brought near him due to the crowds, they went up to the roof and removed the roof tiles of the place where Yeshua was. They let down the litter in which the paralytic lay.
2.5: When Yeshua saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “My son, your sins are forgiven you.”
2.6: But some Scribes and Pharisees sitting there thought in their hearts,
2.7: “Who is this speaking blasphemy? Who is able to forgive sins except God alone?”
2.8: Yeshua knew in his Spirit that they were thinking these things in themselves. He said to them, “Why do you reason these things in your hearts?”
2.9: “What is easier to say to the paralytic: 'Your sins are forgiven you', or to say,'Arise, take your litter, and walk?”
2.10: “But that you may know The Son of Man is authorized to forgive sins in the earth”, he said to the paralytic:
2.11: “I say to you, get up, pick up your bed and go home.”
2.12: And immediately he stood up, picked up his pallet and went out before the eyes of all of them, so that they were all astonished and they glorified God, as they were saying, “We have never seen such a thing!”
2.13: And he went out again to the sea and all the crowds were coming to him and he was teaching them.
2.14: As he passed, he saw Levi, son of Khalphi, sitting down at the place of taxation and he said to him, “Come after me”, and rising he went after him.
2.15: And it was that as he reclined at supper in his house, there were many Tax Collectors and sinners reclining at supper with Yeshua and with his disciples, for many had come, and they were following him.
2.16: And when the Scribes and Pharisees saw him eating with Tax Collectors and with sinners, they said to his disciples, “How is it that he eats and drinks with Tax Collectors and sinners?”
2.17: But when Yeshua heard, he said to them, “The healthy have no need for a physician, but those who have become very ill; I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.”
2.18: But the disciples of Yohannan and the Pharisees had been practicing fasting, and they came and they were saying to him, “Why do the disciples of Yohannan and of the Pharisees fast and your disciples do not fast?”
2.19: Yeshua said to them, “Are the children of the bridal chamber able to fast as long as the groom is with them? No!”
2.20: “But the days will come when the groom will be taken from them; then they will fast in those days.”
2.21: “No man places a new strip of cloth and sews it on an old garment lest the fullness of that new cloth takes from the old, and it rips more.”
2.22: “And no man pours new wine into old wineskins, lest the wine burst the wineskins and the wineskins are destroyed and the wine is spilled; but one pours new wine into new wineskins.”
2.23: And so it was that when Yeshua went out on the Sabbath to a grain field, his disciples were walking and picking the ears of wheat.
2.24: And the Pharisees were saying to him, “Look, why are they doing something that is illegal on the Sabbath?”
2.25: Yeshua said to them, “Have you never read what David did when he was in great need and was hungry, he and his companions,
2.26: As he entered the house of God when Abiathar was Chief Priest, and he ate the bread of the altar of THE LORD JEHOVAH, which was not legal to eat except for the priests? And he gave also to those who were with him.”
2.27: And he said to them, “The Sabbath was created for the sake of man and not man for the sake of the Sabbath.”
2.28: “Therefore The Son of Man is also master of the Sabbath.”

3.1: And Yeshua again entered the synagogue and a certain man was there, whose hand was shriveled.
3.2: And they were watching him, so that if he would heal him on the Sabbath, they might accuse him.
3.3: And he said to that man whose hand was shriveled, “Stand in the center.”
3.4: But he said also to them, “Is it legal to do what is good on the Sabbath or what is evil; to save life or to destroy?” But they were silent.
3.5: And he gazed at them in anger as he was grieved for the callousness of their hearts, and he said to that man, “Stretch out your hand.”, and he reached out and his hand was restored.
3.6: And the Pharisees went forth immediately with some of the house of Herodus and took counsel concerning him how they might destroy him.
3.7: And Yeshua with his disciples went to the sea, and many people from Galilee and from Judea had gone out.
3.8: And great crowds came to him from Jerusalem and from Edom and from the region of Jordan, and from Tyre and from Zidon, because they had heard everything that he had done.
3.9: And he told his disciples that they should bring him a ship because of the crowds, lest they would throng him.
3.10: For he was healing many until they would be falling on him so that they might touch him.
3.11: And those who had plagues from foul spirits, when they saw him, they were falling down and they were crying out and they were saying, “You are the Son of God!”
3.12: And he greatly rebuked them lest they would reveal him.
3.13: And he went up a mountain and he called those whom he wanted and they came to join him.
3.14: And he chose twelve to be with him, and to send them to preach
3.15: And to be authorized to heal the sick and to cast out devils.
3.16: And he gave Shimeon the name Kaypha,
3.17: And to Yaqob, the son of Zebedee and to Yohannan the brother of Yaqob he assigned the name B'nay Regshay, which is B'nay Rama
3.18: And Andreas and Philippus and Bar Tolmay and Mattay and Thoma and Yaqob son of Khalphi and Thadi and Shimeon the Zealot,
3.19: And Yehudah Skariota, he who would betray him, and they came to the house.
3.20: And crowds assembled again so that they were not going to be able to eat bread.
3.21: And his relatives heard and they went out to take hold of him, for they were saying, “He has gone out of his mind.”
3.22: And the Scribes who had come down from Jerusalem were saying, “He has Beelzebub in him and by The Ruler of devils he casts out devils.”
3.23: And Yeshua called them, and he said to them in parables, “How is Satan able to cast out Satan?”
3.24: “For if a kingdom will be divided against itself, that kingdom will not be able to stand.”
3.25: “And if a house will be divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.”
3.26: “And if Satan were to rise up against himself and be divided, he would not be able to stand, but it would be his end.”
3.27: “A man cannot enter the house of a mighty one and rob his valuables, unless he first binds the mighty one, and then he shall plunder his house.”
3.28: “Truly I say to you, all sins and blasphemies that the sons of men will blaspheme shall be forgiven them,”
3.29: “But there is never forgiveness to whoever will blaspheme against The Spirit of Holiness, but he is condemned to eternal judgment;”
3.30: Because they were saying, “He has a foul spirit.”
3.31: And his mother and his brothers came, standing outside, and they sent that they might call him to them.
3.32: But the crowd was sitting around him, and they said to him, “Behold, your mother and your brothers are outside looking for you.”
3.33: And he answered and said to them, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
3.34: And he looked on those who were sitting with him and he said, “Here is my mother and here are my brethren.”
3.35: “For whoever shall do the will of God is my brother and my sister and my mother.”

4.1: But he began teaching again at the seaside and great crowds were gathered to him, so that he would go up and sit in a boat in the sea and all the crowd was standing on land at the shore.
4.2: He was teaching them in many parables, and he said in his teaching:
4.3: “Listen; behold, a sower went out to sow.”
4.4: “And as he sowed, there was some that fell on the side of the road and a bird came and ate it.”
4.5: “But other seed fell on rock where there was not much soil, and immediately it sprouted because there was no depth of soil.”
4.6: “But when the sun arose it was scorched, and because it had no roots, it dried up.”
4.7: “Other fell among the thorns and the thorns came up and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.”
4.8: “But other fell in good ground, and it came up and grew and it yielded fruit, some thirtyfold and some sixtyfold and some an hundred fold.”
4.9: And he said, “Whoever has an ear to hear, let him hear.”
4.10: But when they were by themselves, those twelve who were with him inquired of him about that parable.
4.11: Yeshua said to them, “It has been given to you to know the secrets of the Kingdom of God, but to outsiders, everything has been in parables,”
4.12: “So that seeing they shall see and not see, and hearing they shall hear and they shall not understand, unless perhaps they shall be converted and their sins shall be forgiven them.”
4.13: He said to them, “Do you not know this parable? How shall you know all the parables?”
4.14: “The sower, who sowed, sowed the word.”
4.15: “But these upon the side of the road are those who have the word sown in them, and when they have heard, immediately Satan comes and takes the word away that was sown in their hearts.”
4.16: “And those that were sown upon the rock are those who when they have heard the word immediately receive it with joy.”
4.17: “And they have no root in themselves, but they abide for a while, and when there is distress or persecution because of the word, they are soon subverted.”
4.18: “And those sown among the thorns are those who have heard the word,”
4.19: “And the cares of this world and the seductiveness of wealth and other desires are entering in and choking the word and it becomes unfruitful.”
4.20: “And they that were sown in the good ground are those who hear the word and receive and yield fruit, thirtyfold, sixtyfold and an hundred fold.”
4.21: And he said to them, “Does one bring a lamp to be put under a basket or under a bed? Should it not be put on a lampstand?”
4.22: “For there is nothing covered that shall not be revealed, and nothing secret that will not be discovered.”
4.23: “If a man has an ear to hear, let him hear.”
4.24: And he said to them, “Take heed what you hear; that measure in which you are measuring is measured to you, and it is increased to you who hear.”
4.25: For whoever has it, it shall be given to him; and whoever does not have it, from him shall be taken even that which he has.
4.26: And he said, “Thus is the Kingdom of God like a man who cast seed in the ground.”
4.27: “And he shall sleep and arise by night and by day and the seed grows and lengthens while he is unaware.”
4.28: “For the earth produces fruit itself; first shall be the blade and afterward the ear, then finally the full wheat in the ear.”
4.29: “But whenever the fruit ripens, immediately the sickle is brought because the harvest has arrived.”
4.30: And he said, “to what shall we compare the Kingdom of God and with what parable shall we illustrate it?”
4.31: “It is like a grain of mustard seed, which when it is sown in the ground, is the smallest of all the herb seeds on the earth.”
4.32: “And when it is sown it comes up and becomes greater than all the small herbs and produces great branches, so that the birds will be able to perch in its shade.”
4.33: Yeshua was speaking parables like these with them, parables such as they were able to hear.
4.34: And without parables he was not speaking with them, but he expounded all things to his disciples privately.
4.35: He said to them that day in the evening, “Let us pass over to the other side.”
4.36: And they left the crowd and brought him as he was in the ship, and there were other boats with them.
4.37: And there was a great storm and the wind and the waves were beating into the ship and it was close to being filled.
4.38: But Yeshua was sleeping on a pillow in the stern of the ship and they came and awakened him and they were saying to him, “Our Master, do you not care that we are perishing?!”
4.39: And he arose and rebuked the wind and he said to the sea, “Stop! Shut up!”, and the wind stopped, and there was a great calm.
4.40: And he said to them, “Why are you so fearful? Why do you not have faith?”
4.41: And they were greatly afraid, and they were saying one to another, “Who is this, really, that the wind and sea obey him?”

5.1: And he came to the other side of the sea to the region of the Gadarenes.
5.2: And when he went out from the ship, a man met him from among the tombs that had a foul spirit in him.
5.3: And he dwelt among the tombs and no one was able to bind him with chains,
5.4: Because whenever he had been bound in shackles and chains he had broken the chains and cut the shackles apart and no one was able to subdue him.
5.5: And always at night and in the daytime he was among the tombs and in the mountains and was crying out and cutting himself with stones.
5.6: But when he saw Yeshua from a distance he ran and prostrated himself.
5.7: And he cried out in a loud voice and he said, “What do I have to do with you, Yeshua, Son of God Most High? I adjure you by God that you will not torture me!”
5.8: For he had said to him, “Come out from the man, foul spirit!”
5.9: And he had asked him, “What is your name?” He said to him, “Our name is Legion, for we are many.”
5.10: And he begged of him much that he would not send him out of the region.
5.11: But there was near the mountain a great herd of swine that were feeding.
5.12: And those evil spirits were begging of him and they were saying, “Send us unto those swine that we may enter them.”
5.13: And he allowed them, and these foul spirits went out and entered into the swine, and that herd ran to a precipice and fell into the sea, about 2000 of them, and they drowned in the water.
5.14: And they who were herding them fled and told it in the city, also in the villages, and they went out to see what had happened.
5.15: And they came to Yeshua and they saw him who had been possessed by the evil spirits with him, clothed and reverent, and he who before had the Legion in him was sitting, and they were afraid.
5.16: And those who had seen how it had been with him whom the evil spirits had possessed, reported to them, and also about those swine.
5.17: And they began to ask him to depart from their coast.
5.18: And after he embarked the ship, he whom the evil spirits had possessed was requesting of him that he might remain with him.
5.19: And he did not allow him, but he said to him, “Go to your house to your people and relate to them what THE LORD JEHOVAH has done for you and that he has had compassion upon you.”
5.20: And he departed and he began preaching in the ten cities what Yeshua had done for him, and they were all astounded.
5.21: And after Yeshua had crossed in the ship to the other side, great crowds were again assembled unto him while he was on the seaside.
5.22: And one of The Rulers of the synagogue, whose name was Yorash, came, and when he had seen him, he fell at his feet.
5.23: And he was begging him greatly and he said to him, “My daughter has become very ill; come lay your hand upon her and she will be healed, and she will live.”
5.24: And Yeshua went on with him and a great crowd was close to him and pressing upon him.
5.25: But there was a certain woman who had a flow of blood twelve years
5.26: Who had suffered greatly from many physicians and had spent everything that she had and she had not been helped at all, but she had become even worse.
5.27: When she heard about Yeshua, she came in the press of the crowd from behind him and she touched his garment,
5.28: For she said, “ If I touch even his garment, I shall be saved.”
5.29: And at once the fount of her blood dried up, and she sensed in her body that she was healed of her plague.
5.30: But Yeshua knew in himself at once that power had gone out from him and he turned to the crowds and he said, “Who has touched my garment?”
5.31: And his disciples were saying to him, “You see the crowd pressing against you, and do you say, 'Who has touched me?' “
5.32: And he was gazing that he might see who had done this.
5.33: But that woman, being afraid and trembling, for she knew what had happened to her, came and fell down before him and told him all the truth.
5.34: But he said to her, “My daughter, your faith has saved you; go in peace and be whole from your disease.”
5.35: And while he was speaking, those who were from the house of The Ruler of the synagogue came, and they were saying, “Your daughter has died. Why should you now trouble The Teacher?”
5.36: But Yeshua heard the words which they spoke and he said to The Ruler of the assembly, “Do not fear; only believe.”
5.37: And he allowed no one to go with him except Shimeon Kaypha and Yaqob and Yohannan, brother of Yaqob.
5.38: And they came to The Chief of the assembly's house and he saw those who were disturbed and weeping and wailing.
5.39: And he entered and he said to them, “Why are you disturbed and weeping? The girl is not dead, but she is sleeping.”
5.40: And they were laughing at him, but he put all of them out and he took the father of the girl and her mother, and those who were with him, and he entered the place where the girl was lying.
5.41: And he took the girl's hand and he said to her, “Little girl, arise.”
5.42: Immediately the young girl arose, and she was walking, for she was twelve years old, and they were astounded with great astonishment.
5.43: And he commanded them strictly that they should not relate this to anyone, and he said that they should give her food.

6.1: And he went out from there and he came to the city, and his disciples were staying close to him.
6.2: And when it was The Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue and many who heard were amazed and they were saying, “From where does this man have these things? What is this wisdom given to him that mighty works like these should be done by his hands?”
6.3: “Is this not the carpenter, son of Maryam and brother of Yaqob, and Yose and Yehudah and Shimeon, and are not his sisters here with us?” And they were suspicious of him.
6.4: And Yeshua said to them, “There is no Prophet who is despised except in his city and among his kindred and in his house.”
6.5: And he was not able to do even one powerful work there, but only laid his hands upon a few sick folks and healed them.
6.6: He was astonished at the lack of their faith and he was going around in the villages as he taught.
6.7: And he called his twelve and he began to send them two by two and he gave them authority over vile spirits to cast them out.
6.8: And he commanded them that they would not take anything on the road except perhaps a staff, no bag, no bread, no copper in their purses,
6.9: But that they would wear sandals, and they should not wear two tunics.
6.10: And he said to them, “Into whatever house you enter, stay there until you depart from there.”
6.11: “And all who will not receive you and will not hear you, when you leave that place, shake off the dust that is on the soles of your feet for their testimony; truly I say to you that it will be tranquil for Sadom and Amorah in the day of judgment compared to that city.”
6.12: And they went out and they preached that they should return to God.
6.13: And they were casting out many evil spirits, and they were anointing the many sick with oil and they were healing them.
6.14: And Herodus The King heard about Yeshua, for his name was known to him and he said, “He is Yohannan The Baptizer; he is raised from the grave, therefore, mighty works are performed by him.”
6.15: Others were saying, “He is Elijah”, and others, “He is a Prophet like one of The Prophets.”
6.16: Now when Herodus heard, he said, “Yohannan, whose head I cut off, has arisen from the grave.”
6.17: For Herodus had sent and seized Yohannan and bound him in the prison for the sake of Herodia whom he had taken, the wife of Phillipus his brother.
6.18: For Yohannan had said to Herodus, “It is not lawful for you to take the wife of your brother.”
6.19: But Herodia was threatening him and she wanted to kill him and was unable.
6.20: Herodus had been afraid of Yohannan because he had known that he was a righteous and holy man, and he protected him and he heard many things that he had said and did services for him, and he heard him with pleasure.
6.21: It was a notable day when Herodus made a supper for his nobles and for the Captains and the Leaders of Galilee on his birthday.
6.22: And the daughter of Herodia came in, and she danced, and she pleased Herodus and those who were reclining with him, and The King said to the girl, “Ask of me anything that you desire, and I shall give it to you.”
6.23: And he swore to her, “Anything that you shall ask, I shall give to you, unto the half of my Kingdom.”
6.24: But she went out and said to her mother, “What shall I ask?” She said to her, “The head of Yohannan The Baptizer.”
6.25: At once she entered with diligence to the King, and she said to him, “I want you to give me on a dish, this hour, the head of Yohannan The Baptizer.”
6.26: And it grieved The King much, but because of the oath and because of the dinner guests, he was unwilling to deny her.
6.27: The King sent the executioner at once and commanded to bring the head of Yohannan and he went and cut off the head of Yohannan in the prison.
6.28: And he brought it on a plate and he gave it to the girl and the girl gave it to her mother.
6.29: His disciples heard and they came and took his corpse and laid it in a tomb.
6.30: And The Apostles were gathered unto Yeshua and they told him everything that they had done and everything whatever they had taught.
6.31: And he said to them, “Come, we shall go to the country alone and rest yourselves a little.” For there were many going and coming and there was not a place for them even to eat.
6.32: And they went to a deserted place in a ship by themselves.
6.33: And many saw them as they were going, and they recognized them and they ran on land from all the cities before him to that place.
6.34: And Yeshua went forth, seeing the great crowds, and he was moved with pity on them, for they were like sheep without a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.
6.35: And when the time had grown late, his disciples came to him and they were saying to him, “This is a desolate place and the time is late.”
6.36: “Dismiss them that they may go to the market that they may go around to the villages also and buy bread for themselves, for there is nothing for them to eat.”
6.37: He said to them, “You give them food.” They said to him, “Shall we go buy 200 denarii of bread and give them to eat?”
6.38: He said to them, “Go see how many loaves you have here.” When they looked, they said to him, “Five loaves and two fish.”
6.39: And he commanded them to seat everyone on the grass.
6.40: And they were seated by hundreds and by 50's.
6.41: And he took those five loaves and two fish and gazed into Heaven and he blessed and broke the bread, and he gave to his disciples to set before them and they divided those two fish for all of them.
6.42: And they all ate and they were filled.
6.43: And they took up twelve baskets full of the fragments and also of the fish.
6.44: Now those who ate the bread were 5000 men.
6.45: And at once he urged his disciples to board the ship and go before him to the other side to Bethsaida while he sent the multitude away.
6.46: And when he had sent them away he went to a mountain to pray.
6.47: But when it had become evening, the boat was in the middle of the sea and he was alone on the land.
6.48: And he saw them laboring as they were rowing, for the wind was against them. And it was, that in the fourth watch of the night, Yeshua came toward them as he was walking on the sea, and he wanted to pass by them.
6.49: They saw him that he was walking on the water, and they thought to themselves that he was a hallucination and they cried out,
6.50: For they all saw him and they were afraid and immediately he spoke with them and he said to them, “Take heart, I AM THE LIVING GOD; do not be afraid.”
6.51: And he came up to join them in the ship and the wind ceased, and they were greatly astonished and they wondered in their souls.
6.52: For they had not learned from the bread, because their heart was stupid.
6.53: And when they had crossed to the other side, they came to the land of Genesar.
6.54: And when they went out from the boat, immediately the men of the place recognized him.
6.55: And they ran in all that area, and they began to bring those who had become sick, as they were carrying them in litters to the place where they heard that he was.
6.56: And wherever he had entered a village or city, they were laying the sick in the streets, and they were begging him if they might touch even the fringe of his garment, and all those who were touching it were healed.

7.1: The Pharisees and the Scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around him.
7.2: And they saw some of his disciples eating bread when they had not washed their hands, and they found fault.
7.3: For all the Judeans and the Pharisees do not eat unless they wash their hands carefully because they keep the tradition of the Elders.
7.4: And coming from the marketplace, unless they bathe, they do not eat. And there are many other things which they had received to keep: washings of cups and pots and copper vessels and of beds.
7.5: And the Scribes and Pharisees asked him, “Why do your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the Elders, but eat bread without having washed their hands?”
7.6: But he said to them, “Isaiah the Prophet prophesied beautifully of you impostors, just as it is written: 'This people honors me with its lips, but their heart is very far from Me.'
7.7: 'And in vain they pay reverence to me as they teach doctrines of commandments of the sons of men.'
7.8: “You forsake the commandments of God and you keep the traditions of the sons of men: washings of cups and pots and many such things like these.”
7.9: He said to them, “Well you reject the commandment of God that you may establish your traditions.”
7.10: For Moses said, “Honor your father and your mother”, and “Whoever reviles father and mother shall die the death.”
7.11: But you say, “If a man shall say to his father or to his mother, 'My offering is anything that you shall gain from me.'
7.12: Then you do not allow him to do anything for his father or his mother.
7.13: And you reject the word of God for the traditions that you deliver, and many things like these you do.”
7.14: And Yeshua called to all the crowds, and he said to them, “Hear me all of you and understand.”
7.15: “There is nothing outside of a man that enters into him that can defile him, but the thing that proceeds from him, that is what defiles the man.”
7.16: “Whoever has an ear to hear, let him hear.”
7.17: But when Yeshua entered the house from the crowds, his disciples asked him about that parable.
7.18: He said to them, “Are you also stupid? Do you not know that nothing entering from outside a man can defile him?”
7.19: “Because it does not enter his heart, but his belly, and is discharged by excretion, which purifies all foods.”
7.20: “But the thing that proceeds from a man, that defiles the man.”
7.21: “For from within the heart of the children of men proceed evil ideas, adultery, fornication, theft, murder.”
7.22: “Greed, wickedness, deceit, harlotry, an evil eye, blasphemy, boasting, senselessness.”
7.23: “All these evils proceed from within and defile a man.”
7.24: Yeshua arose from there and came to the coast of Tyre and of Sidon, and he entered a certain house and he did not want anyone to know him and he could not be hidden.
7.25: For immediately a certain woman heard about him, whose daughter had a foul spirit, and she came falling before his feet.
7.26: But she was a pagan woman from Phoenicia of Syria, and she was begging him to cast out the demon from her daughter.
7.27: And Yeshua said to her, “Let the children be filled first, for it is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.”
7.28: But she answered and said to him, “Yes, my lord, but even the dogs from under the table eat the children's crumbs.”
7.29: Yeshua said to her, “Go; because of this saying the demon has gone out from your daughter.”
7.30: And she went to her house and found her daughter lying in bed, and the demon had been cast out of her.
7.31: Yeshua again went out from the coast of Tyre and Sidon, and he came to the Sea of Galilee at the border of The Ten Cities.
7.32: And they brought him a certain deaf man who was dumb and they begged him to lay hands upon him.
7.33: And he took him aside from the crowd alone and put his fingers in his ears and spat and he touched his tongue.
7.34: And he gazed into Heaven and he groaned and he said to him, “Be opened.”
7.35: And at that moment his ears were opened and a bond of his tongue was released and he spoke distinctly.
7.36: And he warned them not to tell anyone, and as much as he warned them, they all the more proclaimed it.
7.37: And they were greatly astonished, and they were saying, “He has done everything beautifully; he has made the deaf to hear and him who was mute to speak.”

8.1: But in those days when there was a great crowd and there was nothing for them to eat, he called his disciples and said to them:
8.2: “I have pity on this crowd, for behold, they have continued with me three days and there is nothing for them to eat.”
8.3: “And if I send them away fasting to their houses they will faint in the road, for some among them have come from far away.”
8.4: His disciples said to him, “From where here in the desert can a man supply enough bread for all of these?”
8.5: He asked them, “How many loaves do you have?, and they said, “Seven.”
8.6: And he commanded the crowds to recline on the ground and he took those seven loaves and blessed and broke and he gave to his disciples to be set out, and they set them out for the crowd.
8.7: And there were a few fish also for them, and he blessed them, and he said to set them out.
8.8: And they ate and were filled and they took up the leftovers of fragments seven baskets.
8.9: But there were about 4000 men who ate.
8.10: And he sent them away and came up at once to the ship with his disciples and came to the region of Dalmanutha.
8.11: And Pharisees came out and they began to inquire with him and they were asking him for a sign from Heaven, while tempting him.
8.12: And he groaned in his spirit and he said, “Why does this generation seek for a sign? Truly I say to you, a sign shall not be given to this generation.”
8.13: And he left them and boarded the ship and they went to the other side.
8.14: They had forgotten to take bread, and except for one cake, there was nothing with them in the ship.
8.15: And he commanded them and he said to them, “Behold, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the leaven of Herodus.”
8.16: And they were reasoning with one another, and they were saying, “It is because we have no bread.”
8.17: But Yeshua knew and he said to them, “Why are you considering that you have no bread? Do you not yet know? Do you not yet understand? Your heart is still hard.”
8.18: “You have eyes and do not see and you have ears and you do not hear, neither do you remember.”
8.19: “When I broke those five loaves for the 5000, after they were filled, how many baskets of fragments did you take up?” They said to him, “Twelve.”
8.20: He said to them, “And when the seven to the 4000, after they were filled, how many baskets of fragments did you take up?” They said, “Seven.”
8.21: He said to them, “How is it that you still do not understand?”
8.22: And he came to Bethsaida, and they brought a blind man to him, and they were begging him to touch him.
8.23: And he grasped the hand of the blind man and took him outside the town and he spat in his eyes and laid his hand upon him and he asked him what he saw.
8.24: He gazed and he said, “I see people like trees that are walking.”
8.25: He laid his hand again on his eyes and he was healed, and he saw everything clearly.
8.26: And he sent him to his house and he said, “Do not enter the town, neither tell anyone in the town.”
8.27: Yeshua and his disciples went out to the villages of Caesarea Philippi, and he was asking his disciples in the road and he said to them, “What is it the people are saying about me as to who I am?”
8.28: Some said, “Yohannan the Baptizer”, and others “Elijah” and others, “One of The Prophets.”
8.29: And Yeshua said to them, “But what is it you are saying about me as to who I am?” Shimeon answered and he said to him, “You are The Messiah, The Son of THE LIVING GOD.”
8.30: And he admonished them that they should not speak about him to anyone.
8.31: And he began to teach them: “The Son of Man is going to suffer many things and to be rejected by the Elders and by the Chief Priests and by the Scribes and he shall be killed, and in three days he shall rise.”
8.32: And he was speaking the word openly and Kaypha took him aside and began to rebuke him.
8.33: But he turned and gazed at his disciples and rebuked Shimeon and said, “Depart behind me Satan, for you do not reason of God, but of the children of men.”
8.34: And Yeshua called the crowds with his disciples, and he said to them, “Whoever is willing to come after me, let him renounce himself and take up his cross and let him come after me.”
8.35: “Whoever desires to save his soul will lose it. Everyone who will lose his life for me and for my gospel will save it.”
8.36: “For how is a man benefited if he should gain the whole world and he should lose his soul?”
8.37: “Or what payment shall a man give to regain his soul again?
8.38: “For everyone who shall be ashamed of me and my words in this sinning and adultery committing generation, of him also shall The Son of Man be ashamed whenever he comes in the glory of his Father with his Holy Angels.”

9.1: And he said to them, “Amen, I say to you, there are men standing here who shall not taste death until they will behold the Kingdom of God coming with wonders.”
9.2: And after six days Yeshua led Kaypha, Yaqob and Yohannan and took them up to a high mountain by themselves and he was transfigured before their eyes.
9.3: And his garment was shining and became very white, like snow, in a manner by which men on earth are not able to whiten.
9.4: And Elijah and Moses appeared to them as they were speaking with Yeshua.
9.5: And Kaypha said to him, “Rabbi, it is beautiful for us to be here; let us make three tabernacles, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
9.6: But he did not know what he was saying, for they were in awe.
9.7: And there was a cloud and it was a shelter about them, and a voice from the cloud which said, “This is my Beloved Son, hear him.”
9.8: And suddenly afterward, the disciples gazed up, and they saw no one except Yeshua alone with them.
9.9: And when they descended from the mountain, he commanded them that they would tell no one anything that they had seen until after The Son of Man had risen from the dead.
9.10: And they seized on that statement in their souls and they were inquiring, “What is this statement, 'When he rises from among the dead?”
9.11: And they asked him and they said, “Why do the Scribes say therefore that Elijah must come first?”
9.12: He said to them, “Elijah does come first, so that he may prepare all things, and just as it is written about The Son of Man, he will suffer much and be rejected.”
9.13: “But I say to you also that Elijah has come and they did to him everything whatever they wished, just as it is written about him.”
9.14: And when he came to the disciples he saw the great crowds among them and the Scribes debating with them.
9.15: And immediately all the crowds saw him and they were astonished and ran and greeted him.
9.16: And he was asking The Scribes, “What are you debating with them?”
9.17: And one from the crowds answered and said, “Teacher, I brought my son to you, who has a dumb spirit.”
9.18: “And whenever it comes upon him, it throws him and it beats him and he gnashes his teeth and he wastes away, and I spoke to your disciples that they might cast it out, and they were not able.”
9.19: Yeshua answered and said to them, “Oh faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long shall I endure you? Bring him to me.”
9.20: And they brought him to him, and when the spirit saw him, immediately he threw him and he fell on the ground and he was convulsed and he foamed.
9.21: Yeshua asked his father, “How long a time now has he been like this? He said to him, “From his childhood.”
9.22: “And many times it has thrown him into fire and into water to destroy him. But if you can do anything, help me, and have mercy on me!”
9.23: Yeshua said to him, “If you are able to believe, everything is possible to the one who believes.”
9.24: And immediately the father of the boy cried out weeping and saying, “I believe my lord; help the lack of my faith.”
9.25: And when Yeshua saw that the people ran and gathered near him, he rebuked that foul spirit and he said to it, “Deaf spirit, that does not speak, I am commanding you, go out from him, and you shall not enter him again.”
9.26: And that demon screamed greatly and pounded him and went out and he was like a dead man, so that many were saying, “He is dead.”
9.27: But Yeshua took him by his hand and raised him up.
9.28: But when Yeshua had entered the house, his disciples asked him among themselves, “Why were we not able to cast it out?”
9.29: He said to them, “This sort cannot come out by anything except by fasting and by prayer.”
9.30: And when he went out from there, they were passing through Galilee, and he did not want anyone to recognize him.
9.31: For he was teaching his disciples and he said to them, “The Son of Man will be delivered into the hands of men and they will kill him, and when he has been killed, on the third day he shall rise.”
9.32: But they did not understand that saying, and they were afraid to ask him.
9.33: And they came to Kapernahum and when they entered the house, he asked them, “What were you reasoning on the road among yourselves?”
9.34: But they were silent, for they had been arguing on the road with one another who was the greatest among them.
9.35: And Yeshua sat down and he called the twelve and he said to them, “He who wants to be first shall be last, and the servant of every person.”
9.36: And he took a certain boy and stood him in the midst and took him up on his shoulders and he said to them:
9.37: “Whoever shall receive one boy like this in my name is receiving me, and whoever receives me has not received me, but him who sent me.
9.38: Yohannan said to him, “Rabbi, we saw a man casting out demons in your name, and we forbade him because he did not go out with us.”
9.39: Yeshua said to them, “Do not forbid him, for there is no man who does a powerful work in my name and can soon speak badly about me.”
9.40: “Whoever therefore is not against you is for you.”
9.41: “But everyone who will give you only a cup of water to drink in the name that you are one who belongs to The Messiah, amen, I say to you, he shall not lose his reward.”
9.42: “And everyone who shall subvert one of these little ones who believe in me, it were better for him if a millstone of a donkey were hung around his neck and he were cast into the sea.”
9.43: “If your hand causes subverts you, cut it off, for it is better for you that you enter the Life maimed, than when you have two hands, you would go to Gehenna.”
9.44: “Where their worm does not die and their fire is not quenched.”
9.45: “If your foot subverts you, cut it off, for it is better for you to enter the Life crippled than when you have two feet that you should fall into Gehenna.”
9.46: “Where their worm does not die and their fire is not quenched.”
9.47: “And if your eye subverts you, pluck it out, for is better for you that you should enter the Kingdom of God with one eye than having two eyes that you should fall into the Gehenna of fire.”
9.48: “Where their worm does not die and their fire is not quenched.”
9.49: “For everything will be seasoned with fire and every sacrifice will be seasoned with salt.”
9.50: “Salt is excellent, but if salt becomes tasteless, with what shall it be seasoned? Have salt in you and be at peace with one another.”

10.1: And he arose from there and came to the borders of Judea to the crossing of the Jordan, and great crowds went there to join him, and he taught them again as he had been accustomed.
10.2: And Pharisees approached, tempting him, and asking, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?”
10.3: He said to them, “What has Moses commanded you?”
10.4: But they said, “Moses allowed us to write a letter of divorce and we may get a divorce.”
10.5: Yeshua answered and he said to them, “He wrote you this commandment confronting the callousness of your heart.”
10.6: “But from the beginning, God made them male and female.”
10.7: “Because of this a man shall leave his father and mother and cleave to his wife.”
10.8: “And the two shall be one flesh; after that they are not two, but one flesh.”
10.9: “The things that God unites let not a son of man separate.”
10.10: And his disciples asked him again in the house about this.
10.11: And he said to them, “Every man who divorces his wife and takes another commits adultery.”
10.12: “And if a woman divorces her husband and another man shall have her, she commits adultery.”
10.13: And they were bringing children to him that he might touch them, but his disciples were rebuking those who brought them.
10.14: But Yeshua saw and was displeased and he said to them, “Let the children come to me and do not forbid them, for the Kingdom of God belongs to such as these.”
10.15: “Amen, I say to you, no one who will not receive the Kingdom of God like a child will enter it.”
10.16: And he took them up in his arms and he laid his hand upon them and he blessed them.
10.17: And as he traveled on the road, one came running and fell on his knees and asked him and said, “Good teacher, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?”
10.18: Yeshua said to him, “Why do you call me good? There is no one good but The One God.”
10.19: “You know the commandments: 'You shall not commit adultery', 'You shall not steal', 'You shall not murder', 'You shall not give false testimony', 'You shall not cheat', 'Honor your father and your mother.' “
10.20: He answered and said to him, “Teacher, I have kept all these things from my childhood.”
10.21: But Yeshua gazed at him and he loved him and he said to him: “You have one thing missing: go sell everything that you have and give to the poor, and you shall have treasure in Heaven, and take the cross and come after me.”
10.22: But he was saddened at this saying and he departed grieving, because he had great wealth.
10.23: But Yeshua gazed at his disciples and he said to them, “How hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the Kingdom of God!”
10.24: But the disciples were astonished at his words. And Yeshua answered again and he said to them, “Children, how hard it is for those who rely upon their wealth to enter the Kingdom of God!”
10.25: “It is easier for a camel to enter into the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the Kingdom of God.”
10.26: But they were all the more astonished, and they were saying among themselves, “Who can be saved?”
10.27: But Yeshua gazed upon them and he said to them, “With the sons of men, this is impossible, but not with God; everything is possible with God.”
10.28: Kaypha began to say, “Behold, we have left everything and have cleaved to you.”
10.29: Yeshua answered and said, “Amen, I say to you, there is no man who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or wife or children or town for my sake and for my Gospel's sake,
10.30: Who will not receive each one an hundredfold now in this time, houses and brothers and sisters and mothers and children and towns, with persecutions, and in the world that is coming, eternal life.
10.31: But many are first who shall be last, and the last first.”
10.32: And as they were going up the road to Jerusalem, Yeshua was in front of them, and they were amazed and they were going after him as they were awe-struck, and he took his twelve, and he began to tell them the things that were going to happen to him:
10.33: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem and The Son of Man will be delivered to the Chief Priests and to the Scribes, and they will condemn him to death and they will deliver him to the Gentiles,
10.34: And they will mock him, and will scourge him, and will spit in his face, and will kill him; and the third day he will arise.”
10.35: And Yaqob and Yohannan, the sons of Zebedee, approached him, and said to him: “Teacher, we desire that you would do for us all that we ask.”
10.36: He said to them: “What do you want me to do for you?”
10.37: They said to him: “Grant to us, that one may sit at your right hand, and the other on your left, in your glory.”
10.38: But he said to them: “You do not know what you ask. Are you able to drink the cup of which I drink, and to be baptized with the baptism in which I am baptized?”
10.39: They were saying to him: “We are able.” Yeshua said to them: “The cup that I drink, you will drink, and in the baptism in which I am baptized, you will be baptized.”
10.40: “But for you to sit at my right and at my left is not mine to give, except to those for whom it is prepared.”
10.41: When the ten heard, they began complaining greatly about Yaqob and Yohannan.
10.42: Yeshua called them and he said to them, “You are aware that those who are considered rulers of the Gentiles are their lords and their great ones have authority over them.”
10.43: “But it will not be so among you, but whoever wants to be great among you will be a servant to you.”
10.44: “And he among you who wants to be the first will be a servant to all.”
10.45: “For even The Son of Man came not to be served, but to serve and give his life a ransom in the place of the many.”
10.46: And they came to Jericho, and when Yeshua went out from Jericho, he and his disciples and the many crowds, Timai, son of Timai, a blind man, was sitting on the side of the road begging,
10.47: And he heard that it was Yeshua the Nazarene, and he began to cry out and to say, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
10.48: And many were rebuking him that he would be quiet, but he was crying out all the more and he said, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
10.49: And Yeshua stood and commanded that they would call him and they called the blind man and they were saying to him, “Take heart, arise, he is calling for you.”
10.50: But that blind man threw off his garment and arose, coming to Yeshua.
10.51: And Yeshua said to him, “What do you want me to do for you?” But the blind man said to him, “Rabbi, that I might see.”
10.52: And Yeshua said to him, “See; your faith has saved you.”, and immediately he saw, and he was going down the road.

11.1: And when he approached Jerusalem on the side of Bayth Phage and Bayth Anya to Tura d'Zaytay, he sent two of his disciples.
11.2: And he said to them, “Go to that village opposite us, and immediately entering you will find a colt that is tied which no one of the children of men has ridden. Loose him and bring him,”
11.3: “And if a man says to you, 'Why are you doing this?', say to him, 'He is needed by Our Lord.' And at once he will send him here.”
11.4: And they went and found a colt that was tied on the gate outside in the street, and as they untied him,
11.5: Some among those who were standing there said to them, “What are you doing that you are untying the colt?”
11.6: But they said to them according to what Yeshua had commanded them, and they allowed them.
11.7: And they brought the colt to Yeshua and they threw their garments on it, and Yeshua rode upon him.
11.8: But many were spreading their coats in the road and others were cutting branches from trees and spreading them in the road.
11.9: And those who were before him and those who were behind were cheering and they were saying, “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of THE LORD JEHOVAH!”
11.10: “Blessed is the Kingdom that comes of our father David! Hosanna in The Highest!”
11.11: And Yeshua entered Jerusalem and into The Temple, and he saw everything, but when it was evening he went out to Beth Anya with the twelve.
11.12: And on the next day when he went out from Bethany he was hungry.
11.13: And he saw a fig tree from a distance that had leaves on it and he came to it that perhaps he might find something on it, and when he came, he found nothing on it but leaves, for it was not the season of figs.
11.14: And he said to it, “From now and forever no man will eat fruit from you!” And his disciples heard it and they came to Jerusalem.
11.15: And Yeshua entered The Temple of God and he began to cast out those who bought and sold in The Temple and he overturned the tables of the money exchangers and the seats of those who were selling doves.
11.16: And he allowed no one to carry merchandise inside The Temple.
11.17: And he taught and he said to them, “Is it not written: 'My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations?' But you have made it a den of robbers.”
11.18: And the Chief Priests and the Scribes heard and they were seeking how they might destroy him, for they were afraid of him, because all the people were awestruck at his teaching.
11.19: And when it was evening, they went outside the city.
11.20: And in the morning when they were passing by, they saw the fig tree after it had dried up from its roots.
11.21: And Shimeon remembered and he said to him, “Rabbi, look, that fig tree that you cursed has dried up!”
11.22: Yeshua answered and he said to them, “May the faith of God be in you.”
11.23: “For, amen, I say to you, whoever shall say to this mountain: 'Be lifted up and fall into the sea', and will not doubt in his heart but shall believe that thing which he says, anything that he says shall be done for him.”
11.24: “Therefore I say to you, that everything that you pray and ask, believe that you are receiving it and you shall have it.”
11.25: “And whenever you stand to pray, forgive whatever you have against anyone, so that your Father who is in Heaven may also forgive you your faults.”
11.26: “But if you are not forgiving, neither will your Father in Heaven forgive you your faults.”
11.27: And they came again to Jerusalem and when he was walking in The Temple, the Chief Priests and the Scribes came to him, and the Elders.
11.28: And they were saying to him, “By what authority are you doing these things? And who gave you this authority to do these things?”
11.29: But Yeshua said to them, “I will ask you also one matter that you should tell me and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.”
11.30: “From where was the baptism of Yohannan, from Heaven, or from the sons of men? Tell me.”
11.31: And they reasoned in themselves and they said, “If we say to him from Heaven, he will say to us, 'And why did you not believe him?' “
11.32: “And that we should say, 'From the sons of men', there is the fear of the people, for they all are holding that Yohannan was truly a Prophet.”
11.33: And they answered and they were saying to Yeshua, “We do not know.” And he said to them, “Neither am I telling you by what authority I do these things.”

12.1: And he began to speak with them in a parable: “A certain man planted a vineyard and enclosed it with a hedge and dug a wine press in it and built a tower into it and gave its care to laborers, and he went abroad.”
12.2: “And he sent his servant to the workers in time to receive the fruits of the vineyard.”
12.3: “But they beat him and sent him away empty.”
12.4: “And he sent another servant to them again and they threw rocks at him and cut him, and they sent him away shamefully.”
12.5: “And again he sent another also and they murdered him; he sent many other servants, and some of them they beat and they murdered some of them.”
12.6: “Finally, he had one beloved son, and he sent him to them afterward, for he said, 'Doubtless, they will be ashamed before my son.'”
12.7: “But those laborers said within themselves, 'This is the heir; come, we will kill him and the inheritance will be ours.'”
12.8: “And they took and murdered him and cast him outside of the vineyard.”
12.9: “What therefore will the Lord of the vineyard do? He will come destroy those laborers and will give the vineyard to others.”
12.10: “Have you not even read this scripture: 'The stone that the builders rejected has become the head of the corner,' “
12.11: “'This One was from the presence of THE LORD JEHOVAH, and he is wonderful in our eyes?
12.12: And they were seeking to seize him and they were afraid of the people, for they knew that he had spoken this parable about them and they left him and departed.
12.13: And they sent men to him from the Scribes and from the followers of Herodus to trap him in his words.
12.14: But those came and asked him, “Teacher, we know that you are true, and that you are not motivated by care for any man, for you do not regard the person of the sons of men, but you teach the way of God in truth. Is it lawful to give head tax to Caesar or not? Should we give or should we not give?”
12.15: But he knew their treachery and he said to them, “Why are you testing me? Bring me a penny that I may see it.”
12.16: And they brought it; he said to them: “Whose is this image and writing?” They said, “Caesar's.”
12.17: Yeshua said to them, “Give what is Caesar's to Caesar and what is God's to God.” And they marveled at him.
12.18: And the Sadducees came to him, who say there is no resurrection, and they were asking him and they were saying:
12.19: “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man's brother dies and he leaves a wife and leaves behind no sons, his brother shall take his wife and raise up seed for his brother.”
12.20: “There were seven brothers; the first took a wife and he died and left no son behind.”
12.21: “And the second took her and he died, having also not left any son behind, and the third likewise.”
12.22: “And the seven of them took her and they left no seed after any of them, and the woman died also.”
12.23: “Therefore in the resurrection, whose wife among them will she be, for the seven had taken her?”
12.24: Yeshua said to them, “Do you not therefore err, because you do not know the scriptures, nor the mighty work of God?”
12.25: For when they have arisen from the dead, they do not take wives, neither do men have wives, but they are like the Angels in Heaven.”
12.26: “But concerning the dead, that they rise, have you not read in the scripture of Moses how God said to him from the Bush, 'I AM THE LIVING GOD, The God of Abraham, and the God of Isaaq and the God of Jaqob?'”
12.27: “He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You therefore are greatly mistaken.”
12.28: One of the Scribes approached him and he heard them debating and he saw that he had answered them beautifully and he asked him, “Which is the first commandment of all?”
12.29: Yeshua said to him, “The first of all the commandments: 'Hear Israel, THE LORD JEHOVAH your God, THE LORD JEHOVAH, he is One.' “
12.30: “'And you shall love THE LORD JEHOVAH your God with your whole heart and with your entire soul and with your entire mind and with all your power.' This is the first commandment”
12.31: “And the second is like it: 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself.' There is no other commandment greater than these.”
12.32: And that scribe said to him, “Excellent, Rabbi! You have spoken the truth, for he is One, and there is not another outside of him.”
12.33: “And for a man to love him with all the heart and with the entire mind and with all the soul and with all the strength, and to love his neighbor as himself, is more than all burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
12.34: But Yeshua, seeing that he had answered wisely, answered and said to him, “You are not far from the Kingdom of God.” And no man again dared to question him.
12.35: And Yeshua answered and said as he taught in The Temple, “How are the Scribes saying that The Messiah is the son of David?”
12.36: “For David himself said in The Spirit of Holiness: 'THE LORD JEHOVAH said to my Lord, “Sit at my right until I shall constitute your enemies as a footstool under your feet.'”
12.37: “David therefore called him, 'My Lord'', and how is he his son?” And the whole crowd was listening to him with pleasure.
12.38: And in his teaching, he said to them, “Beware of the Scribes who like to walk in robes and love greetings in the streets,
12.39: And the best seats in the synagogue and top rooms at feasts,
12.40: They who devour widows' houses by an offering for making their long prayers; they shall receive the greater judgment.”
12.41: And when Yeshua sat down near the treasury, he beheld how the crowds cast money into the treasury and many rich men were casting in much
12.42: And one poor widow came and cast in two minas that were shemonas.
12.43: But Yeshua called his disciples and he said to them, “Amen, I say to you, this poor widow has cast in more than all the people who have cast into the treasury,
12.44: For they all cast in from the surplus that they had, but this one has cast in from her want, everything that she had; she cast in all her possessions.”

13.1: And when Yeshua went out from The Temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, look, see what stones and what buildings!”
13.2: But Yeshua said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There is not left here one stone standing upon another that will not be pulled down.”
13.3: And when Yeshua sat down at Tura D' Zaytay opposite The Temple, Kaypha and Yaqob and Yohannan and Andraeus asked him alone:
13.4: “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign when all these things are near to being fulfilled?”
13.5: But Yeshua himself began to say to them, “Take heed lest a man shall lead you astray.”
13.6: “For many will come in my name, and they will say, 'I AM THE LIVING GOD', and they will deceive many.”
13.7: “Whenever you hear war and reports of battles, you should not be afraid; that is going to happen, but it is not yet the end.”
13.8: “For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places, and there will be famines and sedition; these are the beginning of the labor pains.”
13.9: “But take heed to yourselves, for they will deliver you to Judges and you will be scourged in their synagogues and you will stand before Kings and Governors for my sake as a testimony to them.”
13.10: “But first my gospel is going to be preached in all the nations.”
13.11: “Whenever they bring you to deliver you up, you should not be anxious beforehand what you will speak, neither should you consider, but whatever is given to you in that hour, speak, for you will not be speaking, but The Spirit of Holiness.”
13.12: “But brother will deliver his brother to death and a father his son; children will rise up against their parents and put them to death.”
13.13: “And you will be hated by everyone because of my name, but whoever will endure until the end will be saved.”
13.14: “But whenever you see the unclean sign of desolation, which was spoken of by Daniel the Prophet, standing where it ought not ( He who reads, let him perceive ) then those who are in Judea should flee to the mountains.”
13.15: “Whoever is on the rooftop should not come down and should not enter to take anything from his house.”
13.16: “And whoever is in the field should not turn to go back to take up his garment.”
13.17: “But woe to the pregnant and to those who are nursing in those days.”
13.18: “Pray that your flight will not be in the winter.”
13.19: “For there will be distress in those days, the likes of which has never been since the beginning of the creation which God has created until now, and never will be.”
13.20: “And if THE LORD JEHOVAH had not shortened those days, no one would live; but for the sake of the chosen ones whom he has chosen, he has shortened those days.”
13.21: “Then, if a man shall say to you, 'Look, here is The Messiah, and 'Look, he is going there', you should not believe him.”
13.22: “And false Messiahs and lying Prophets will arise, and they will give signs and wonders and they will deceive if possible, even the chosen ones.”
13.23: “But pay attention; behold, I have anticipated this; I have told you everything.”
13.24: “But in those days, after that distress, the sun will darken and the moon will not give its light.”
13.25: “And the stars will fall from the heavens and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.”
13.26: “And then they will see The Son of Man when he comes in the clouds with great power and with glory.”
13.27: “Then he will send his Angels and gather his elect ones from the four winds and the bottom of the earth even unto the top of Heaven.”
13.28: “But learn an allegory from the fig tree: whenever its branches are tender and its leaves bud, you know that summer has arrived.”
13.29: “Thus also whenever you see these things happen, know that it is near, at the door.”
13.30: “Amen, I say to you, this generation will not pass until all of these things will come to pass.”
13.31: “The heavens and the earth will pass away, and my words will not pass away.”
13.32: But of that day and of that hour no man knows, neither the Angels of Heaven, neither The Son, but The Father only.”
13.33: “Take heed, watch and pray, for you do not know when that time is.”
13.34: “It is like a man who traveled abroad and left his house and gave authority to his servants and to each man his work and he commanded the gatekeeper to be watchful.”
13.35: “Watch therefore, for you do not know when the Master of the house comes, in the evening or at midnight or at cockcrow or in the morning.”
13.36: “Lest suddenly he come and find you sleeping.”
13.37: “But what I say to you, I say the same to you all: 'Be watching.' “

14.1: But after two days was the Passover of Unleavened Bread, and the Chief Priests and Scribes were seeking how they would seize him by treachery and kill him.
14.2: And they were saying, “Not during the feast, lest there be a tumult among the people.”
14.3: And while he was in Bethany in the House of Shimeon the Potter, as he was reclining, a woman came who had with her an alabaster vase of ointment of the best spikenard, very expensive, and she opened it and poured it on Yeshua's head.
14.4: But there were some of the disciples who were displeased within themselves and they said, “Why the loss of this ointment?”
14.5: “It would have been possible to sell this for more than 300 denarii and for it to be given to the poor.” And they were indignant at her.
14.6: But Yeshua said, “Let her alone. Why are you troubling her? She has done a beautiful deed for me.”
14.7: “For the poor are always with you, and whenever you wish, you can do good for them, but I am not always with you.”
14.8: “She has done what she could, and she has anticipated anointing my body for burial.”
14.9: And amen, I say to you, that everywhere that this my gospel shall be preached in all the world, this thing that she has done shall also be spoken in her memory.”
14.10: But Yehuda Skariota, one of the twelve, went to the Chief Priests to betray Yeshua to them,
14.11: But when they heard, they rejoiced, and they promised they would give money to him and he sought an opportunity to betray him.
14.12: And on the first day of Unleavened Bread, in which the Jews slaughter the Passover lamb, his disciples were saying to him, “Where do you want us to go to prepare for you that you may eat the Passover?”
14.13: And he sent two of his disciples and said to them, “Go to the city and behold, you will meet a man among you, bearing a vessel of water. Follow him.”
14.14: “And in the place that he enters, say to the owner of the house, “Our Master says, 'Where is the house chamber, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?' “
14.15: “And behold, he will show you a great upper room, laid out and prepared; there make ready for us.”
14.16: And his disciples went out and they came to the city, and they found just as he told them, and they prepared the Passover.
14.17: And when it was evening he came with his twelve.
14.18: And as they reclined and ate, Yeshua said, “Amen, I say to you, one of you who eats with me will betray me.”
14.19: But they began to lose heart, and they were saying to him, each one, “Is it I?”
14.20: But he said to them, “One of the twelve who dips with me in the dish.”
14.21: “The Son of Man goes just as it is written about him, but woe to that man by whose hand The Son of Man is betrayed. It were better for that man if he had not been born.”
14.22: And while they were eating, Yeshua took bread, and he blessed and he broke and he gave to them, and he said to them, “Take; this is my body.”
14.23: And he took the cup, and he gave thanks and he blessed; and he gave to them and they all drank from it.
14.24: And he said to them, “This is my blood of the new covenant, which is shed in exchange for the many.”
14.25: “Amen, I say to you, I shall not drink from the fruit of the vine again until the day in which I shall drink it anew in the Kingdom of God.”
14.26: And they sang praises, and they went out toTur Zaytay.
14.27: And Yeshua said to them, “All of you will be ashamed of me this night, for it is written: 'I shall strike The Shepherd, and his lambs will be scattered.' “
14.28: “But when I am risen, I shall go before you to Galilee.”
14.29: And Kaypha said to him, “If all of them will be subverted, yet I shall not.”
14.30: Yeshua said to him, “Amen, I say to you that today, this night, before the rooster will crow two times, you will renounce me three times.”
14.31: But he was saying all the more, “If I am to be put to death with you, I shall not renounce you, my Lord!” And like him also, all of them spoke.
14.32: And they came to the place which is called Gethsemane, and he said to his disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”
14.33: And he took Kaypha and Yaqob and Yohannan with him, and he began to be saddened and languid.
14.34: And he said to them, “My soul is saddened unto death. Stay here and watch”
14.35: And he approached a little and he fell on the ground, and he prayed that if it were possible, the hour would pass from him.
14.36: And he said, “Father, my Father, you can do everything; let this cup pass from me, yet not my own will, but yours.”
14.37: And he came and found them while they were sleeping and he said to Kaypha, “Shimeon, are you sleeping? Were you not able to watch one hour?”
14.38: “Watch and pray lest you enter into temptation. The spirit is willing and ready, but the body is weak.”
14.39: And he went again and prayed, and he said the same words.
14.40: And returning, he came again and found them sleeping because their eyes were heavy, and they did not know what to say to him.
14.41: And he came the third time and he said to them, “Sleep now and take rest; the end has arrived; the hour has come; behold The Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.”
14.42: “Arise, let us go; behold, he who betrays me is approaching.”
14.43: And while he was speaking, Yehuda Skariota came, one of the twelve, and many men with swords and clubs from the presence of the Chief Priests and the Scribes and the Elders.
14.44: And the traitor who betrayed him had given them a sign and he said, “He whom I shall kiss is the one; seize him securely and bring him.”
14.45: And immediately he approached and he said to him, “Rabbi, Rabbi”, and he kissed him.
14.46: But those laid hands upon him and they seized him.
14.47: One of those who were standing drew a sword and hit the servant of The High Priest and took off his ear.
14.48: But Yeshua answered and said to them, “Have you gone out as against a band of robbers with swords and with clubs to seize me?”
14.49: “I was with you every day when I taught in The Temple, and you did not seize me. But this has happened that the Scriptures may be fulfilled.”
14.50: Then his disciples forsook him and they fled.
14.51: And one young man had come after him and was clothed with a cloth. He was naked, and they seized him.
14.52: But he left the cloth and fled naked.
14.53: They brought Yeshua to Qaiapha the High Priest, and all the Chief Priests and the Scribes and the Elders gathered to him.
14.54: But Shimeon was coming from a distance after him until within the courtyard of The High Priests, and he sat down with the servants and warmed himself near the fire.
14.55: But the Chief Priests and all their assembly were seeking testimony against Yeshua, that they might put him to death, and they were not able.
14.56: For many were testifying against him, but their testimony was not worthy.
14.57: But people rose up against him and they were testifying lies, and they said:
14.58: “We have heard him say, 'I shall destroy this temple that is made with hands and in three days I shall build another that is not made with hands.'”
14.59: But likewise, neither was their testimony worthy.
14.60: The High Priest stood up in the center and asked Yeshua, and he said, “Do you not answer? Why are these testifying against you?”
14.61: But he was silent, and he did not answer him anything. And again, The High Priest asked him and said, “Are you The Messiah, The Son of The Blessed One?”
14.62: But Yeshua said to him, “I AM THE LIVING GOD, and you shall behold The Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of Heaven.”
14.63: But The High Priest ripped his tunic and he said, “Why now do we need witnesses?”
14.64: “Behold, you have heard the blasphemy from his own mouth. How does it appear to you?” But they all judged that he deserved death.
14.65: And the people began spitting in his face and covering his face and hitting him, and they were saying, “Prophesy”, and the guards were hitting him on his jaw.
14.66: And while Shimeon was below in the courtyard, a certain handmaid of The High Priest came.
14.67: She saw him warming himself and she stared at him and she said to him, “You also were with Yeshua the Nazarene.”
14.68: But he denied it and he said, “I don't know what you are saying,”, and he went forth outside to the porch and a rooster crowed.
14.69: And that handmaid saw him again and she began to say to those who were standing there, “This is also one of them.”
14.70: But he again denied it and after a little while again those who were standing said to Kaypha, “Truly, you are one of them, for you are also a Galilean and your speech is similar.”
14.71: But he had begun cursing and swearing: “I do not know this man of whom you speak.”
14.72: And at that moment, the cock crowed the second time, and Shimeon was reminded of the word of Yeshua, who had said to him, “Before a cock shall crow two times, you will deny me three times”, and he began to weep.

15.1: And at once in the morning the Chief Priests made a council with the Elders and with the Scribes and with the whole assembly, and they bound Yeshua and brought him and delivered him to Pilate.
15.2: And Pilate asked him, “ You are The King of the Judeans?” But he answered and said to him, “You have said.”
15.3: And the Chief Priests accused him of many things.
15.4: Then Pilate again asked him and said to him, “Do you not answer? See how many are testifying against you!”
15.5: But Yeshua gave no answer, to Pilate's amazement.
15.6: But he had set a time at every feast to release one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
15.7: And there was one who was called Barabba, who was bound with those who had made sedition, who had committed murder in the sedition.
15.8: And the people cried out and they began to ask that he would keep the custom which he had been doing for them.
15.9: And Pilate answered and said, “Are you willing that I release to you The King of the Judeans?”
15.10: For Pilate had known that for envy the Chief Priests had delivered him to them.
15.11: But the Chief Priests urged the crowd all the more that he would release Barabba.
15.12: But Pilate said to them, “What do you want me to do to this one whom you call The King of the Judeans?”
15.13: But they again cried out, “Crucify him!”
15.14: But Pilate said to them, “What evil has he done?” And they were crying out even more, “Crucify him!”
15.15: But Pilate chose to do the will of the crowds and he released Barabba to them and delivered Yeshua to them, after he had been scourged, to be crucified.
15.16: But the soldiers led him within the courtyard, which is the Praetorium, and they called the whole company of soldiers.
15.17: And they clothed him in purple and they wound and placed on him a crown of thorns.
15.18: And they began to greet him: “Hail, King of the Judeans!”
15.19: And they were hitting him on his head with a reed and spitting in his face and bowing on their knees and worshiping him.
15.20: And when they had mocked him, they stripped him of the purple and clothed him in his clothes and led him to crucify him.
15.21: And they detained one who was passing by, Shimeon, a Cyrenian, who had come from the field, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to take up his cross.
15.22: And they brought him to the place Gagultha, which is interpreted, “The Skull.”
15.23: And they gave him wine to drink, mixed with myrrh, but he would not take it.
15.24: And when they crucified him, they divided his garments and cast lots for them, who would take what.
15.25: But it was the third hour when they crucified him.
15.26: And the pretext of his death was written in this writing: “This is The King of the Judeans.”
15.27: And they crucified two robbers with him, one at his right, and one at his left.
15.28: And the scripture was fulfilled that says, “He was numbered with wicked men.”
15.29: But those also who were passing by were blaspheming against him and were shaking their heads and they were saying, “Oh destroyer of The Temple and the one who builds it in three days,”
15.30: “Save yourself and come down from the cross!”
15.31: And likewise the Chief Priests were joking with one another and the Scribes and they were saying, “He saved others; he cannot save himself!”
15.32: “Let The Messiah, The King of Israel, descend now from the cross that we may see and we will believe in him”. And those also who were crucified with him were reviling him.
15.33: And when it was the sixth hour, darkness was over all the earth until the ninth hour.
